title: General n-term QM-AM inequality (squared-sum form)
informal_statement: For real-valued f on an arbitrary Finset s, (sum_{i in s} f_i)^2 <= |s| * (sum_{i in s} f_i^2) -- the general n-term QM-AM inequality, equivalent to Cauchy-Schwarz applied against a constant-1 vector.
informal_proof_idea: Direct application of Mathlib's sq_sum_le_card_mul_sum_sq, the library's general finite squared-sum bound.
formal_statement_pp: theorem general_qm_am (ι : Type) (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → ℝ) : (∑ i ∈ s, f i) ^ 2 ≤ (s.card : ℝ) * ∑ i ∈ s, (f i) ^ 2

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 0916c5cb), kernel_verified on the second attempt. Extends `qm_am_bound`/`three_var_qm_am_bound`'s fixed small arities to the general n-term/Finset case, completing the same 'general_X' ladder-extension pattern as `general_amgm` and `general_cauchy_schwarz` this session. First attempt tried `Finset.sq_sum_le_card_mul_sum_sq` (namespaced, matching the `mathlib_search_declarations` file path `Mathlib/Algebra/Order/Chebyshev.lean`), which failed with 'unknown constant' -- the lemma is actually NOT under the `Finset` namespace despite operating on `Finset.sum`; the bare name `sq_sum_le_card_mul_sum_sq` (no prefix) resolved correctly on the second attempt. Worth remembering: a Finset-indexed lemma is not always declared inside the `Finset` namespace.
